Evidence provided: R(Pi) is computed from a positive modal series and checked against finite-difference and finite-element solutions.
Allowed scope: Applies to the stated one-dimensional saturated shear-band, boundary-condition and source assumptions.
Main falsifier or limitation: A coupled or monitored case with nonuniform source, anisotropy or three-dimensional drainage should be analysed with a site-specific model.
Required escalation: Escalate to coupled hydro-mechanical analysis rather than treating R(Pi) as a universal constitutive law.

Evidence provided: Thresholds R(Pi)=0.9, 0.5 and 0.1 are computed from the same operator and audited with truncation-error bounds.
Allowed scope: Use as a screening boundary between nearly undrained, partly drained and drained response.
Main falsifier or limitation: Do not use the thresholds as calibrated field limits without independent hydraulic and geometric data.
Required escalation: Report h, cv, T, boundary condition and source history whenever the classification is used.

Evidence provided: The retained pressure is inserted into an effective-stress factor of safety for synthetic infrastructure-slope cases.
Allowed scope: Supports transparent comparison of drainage assumptions in screening inventories.
Main falsifier or limitation: Does not replace site-specific strength selection, progressive-failure analysis or calibrated pore-pressure modelling.
Required escalation: Use the diagnostic as a pre-analysis check before full design calculations.

Evidence provided: Oso ring-shear records, Mount Kaba-san pore-pressure records and flume observations are treated as bounded consistency checks.
Allowed scope: Supports plausibility of the pressure-dissipation component, not field-scale landslide prediction.
Main falsifier or limitation: A field validation claim would require observed geometry, hydraulic properties, pore-pressure forcing and slope response for the same event.
Required escalation: Label these outputs as consistency checks unless a full field calibration is added.
